title: Pentacalia tonduzii
taxonomy: Pentacalia tonduzii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Central America, specifically in Costa Rica and Panama. The plant belongs to the genus Pentacalia, which is a group of plants commonly known as the "five-part" plants due to their distinctive leaf arrangement. This genus is further classified under the subfamily Vernonioideae and tribe Vernonieae. Pentacalia tonduzii is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within this classification.
uses: Pentacalia tonduzii is a plant species with potential medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. Its leaves and stems have been used to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have been studied for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
